_provenance.runtime_constraint: All four cross-stage reviews (2,3,4,5) ran as in-house simulated reviews under fresh-context subagents because the runtime had no external Gemini/ChatGPT/DeepSeek/Grok access. Model diversity is NOT achieved. Independent-context pressure IS achieved. Every correction below is self-verified against the brief and Stage 1; any introduced specifics are flagged INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified). The user must still run the real cross-vendor rounds before publication if model-diversity matters for this issue.

revisions_applied.from: Singapore charged three over Nvidia chips routed via Malaysia. Johor kept building.
revisions_applied.to: Singapore charged three over Nvidia chips routed via Malaysia. Johor's boom runs on the same chips.
revisions_applied.rationale: Stage 2 flagged 'Johor kept building' as misleading sequencing — implies reactive defiance when the events are concurrent and structurally independent. Rephrased to a structural-dependence framing, which is verifiable (Johor's DCs do run on the same US-controlled AI chips) without claiming a post-charge approval surge.

revisions_applied.from: On 27 Feb 2025, Singapore charged three men with fraud tied to servers carrying Nvidia AI chips that moved Singapore to Malaysia. Johor has approved more data-centre capacity since. The US export-control framework over those chips is still being rewritten.
revisions_applied.to: On 27 Feb 2025, Singapore charged three men with fraud tied to servers carrying Nvidia AI chips that moved Singapore to Malaysia. Johor's data-centre boom runs on chips governed by a US export-control framework still being rewritten.
revisions_applied.rationale: Same Stage 2 catch — removed the implicit 'approved more since' framing in favour of structural-dependence framing.

revisions_applied.from: On 27 February 2025, Singapore charged three men over the sale of servers carrying Nvidia AI chips. The route ran Singapore to Malaysia.
revisions_applied.to: Singapore charged three men over Nvidia AI server sales on 27 Feb 2025: a Singaporean, a permanent resident, a Chinese national. The route ran Singapore to Malaysia.
revisions_applied.rationale: Stage 5 flagged 'three men' as UNDERCLAIM. The citizenship categories (citizen, PR, Chinese national) were in the brief and in widely-reported coverage of the SPF announcement; the brief flagged exact identities as needing verification but the categories themselves are at a lower bar. Adding categories adds concreteness without introducing unverified detail. Stage 3 (no-web-search) should re-verify the categories against SPF charge-sheet wording before publication.

revisions_applied.from: US prosecutors are investigating whether chips that landed in Malaysia were diverted to a Chinese AI firm. Johor approved more capacity that quarter.
revisions_applied.to: US prosecutors are separately probing whether chips that landed in Malaysia reached a Chinese AI firm. Johor's data-centre boom runs on the same supply chain.
revisions_applied.rationale: Removed the 'approved more capacity that quarter' framing (Stage 2 sequencing flag, Stage 5 pre/post-charge split is not in the brief). Replaced with the structural-dependence framing that is in the brief.

revisions_applied.from: Washington classifies Malaysia as a 'Tier 2' diversion-risk country for AI chips, even after the rule was rescinded.
revisions_applied.to: The 'Tier 2' label came from a rule that never took effect. Case-by-case licensing replaced it, but the diversion-risk posture toward Malaysia stayed.
revisions_applied.rationale: Stage 4 and Stage 5 both flagged that anchoring on Tier 2 past the 13 May 2025 rescission overstates Malaysia-specific stigma — the regulation that created the label was rescinded two days before its scheduled effect. The corrected framing keeps the substantive point (US posture treats Malaysia as a diversion-risk waypoint) without overclaiming the formal Tier 2 status.

revisions_applied.from: BIS issued the AI Diffusion Rule on 13 Jan 2025 placing Malaysia in Tier 2. Lutnick rescinded it 13 May 2025 and replaced it with case-by-case licensing plus a diversion warning.
revisions_applied.to: BIS issued the Diffusion Rule on 13 Jan 2025. Lutnick rescinded it 13 May 2025, two days before its scheduled effect, and switched to case-by-case licensing.
revisions_applied.rationale: Tightened. The 'two days before its scheduled effect' specific is from the brief and reinforces that the rule never operated. The 'diversion warning' clause moved to the big to keep the meaning.

revisions_applied.from: Johor has 80+ approved data-centre projects and over 5 GW of announced capacity, roughly a quarter of Peninsular Malaysia's peak power demand.
revisions_applied.to: Johor has 80+ announced data-centre projects and over 5 GW in announced capacity, about a quarter of Peninsular peak demand if all are built.
revisions_applied.rationale: Stage 5 caught the announced-vs-operational conflation. Added 'announced' twice and 'if all are built' to the denominator-comparison. Stage 3 also flagged the >80 and >5 GW figures as not independently re-verified against the latest MIDA Annual Investment Performance Report — left in the card with the brief's own range disclosure intact; Stage 3 (real web) priority before publication.

revisions_applied.from: Microsoft (USD 2.2B), Google (USD 2B), AWS, ByteDance and YTL are anchor tenants. The thesis assumes uninterrupted access to US-controlled AI chips.
revisions_applied.to: YTL operates Sedenak Tech Park; ByteDance and GDS have Johor footprints. The thesis assumes US-controlled AI chips keep flowing.
revisions_applied.rationale: Stage 3 caught the critical MISLEADING_FRAMING — Google's USD 2 B announcement (30 May 2024) is for Selangor (Elmina), not Johor; Microsoft's USD 2.2 B (May 2024) is Greater KL, not Johor-specific; AWS's commitments are also more regional. Listing all five under a 'Johor has' card silently transferred Johor-specificity to operators that don't have it. Replaced with named Johor-anchored operators only (YTL/Sedenak, ByteDance, GDS) — all of which are in the brief. The combined-USD-figure removal is a small loss of concreteness; the trade against accuracy is unambiguous.

revisions_applied.from: MITI said it would investigate any Malaysian company named in a US diversion probe. No list has been made public.
revisions_applied.to: Fourteen months after MITI promised to investigate any Malaysian company named in a US probe, no Malaysian company has been publicly named.
revisions_applied.rationale: Both Stage 2 and Stage 5 flagged this as UNDERCLAIM. 'No list has been made public' implies a private list may exist; the evidence supports the stronger statement that no MY company has been publicly named at all. Reframed to lead with the 14-month enforcement vacuum (verifiable arithmetic from the brief's own timeline: Mar 2025 commitment → May 2026 cutoff = 14 months).

revisions_applied.from: Trade Minister Tengku Zafrul gave that commitment in March 2025. The Strategic Trade Act 2010 carries fines to RM1M and 5 years' jail for unlicensed strategic-item export.
revisions_applied.to: Tengku Zafrul (then MITI) made the commitment in March 2025. The Strategic Trade Act 2010 caps unlicensed-export at RM1M and 5 years' jail.
revisions_applied.rationale: Stage 3 noted the STA 2010 body-corporate maximum may be higher than RM1M; the brief itself lists 'corporate fine up to RM 1 million / individual up to 5 years' imprisonment'. Kept the brief's framing. Tightened wording slightly.

revisions_applied.from: Johor's data-centre boom and Singapore's chip case tell one story: Washington decides which chips Malaysia gets to run.
revisions_applied.to: Johor's data-centre boom and Singapore's chip case tell one story. Putrajaya can use its own Strategic Trade Act, or wait for Washington to act first.
revisions_applied.rationale: Stage 4 (alt-framing) was sharp here: the v1 reframe rotated the model toward US agency but stopped short. The corrected version rotates the model toward Malaysian agency — Putrajaya has STA 2010 and Customs Act tools, choosing not to use them is the news. This is the 'value restructuring' form of reframe (per the playbook), shifting the moral category from 'we're a passive node' to 'we have agency we are not exercising'.
Stage 5 explicitly noted the analogy mirrors verified facts and is clearly labelled editorial. No revision.

revisions_applied.from: Singapore charged three men in February. The chips moved through Malaysia. Johor approved more capacity. Washington decides what happens next.
revisions_applied.to: Singapore charged three men. The chips moved through Malaysia. Fourteen months on, no Malaysian case has been announced. Washington still has the move.
revisions_applied.rationale: Stage 4 and Stage 5 both flagged the v1 view as conceding too much agency to Washington and as implying a post-charge approval link. The corrected view keeps the structural-exposure thesis but lands on the verifiable 14-month enforcement vacuum (the editorial finding) rather than a vague 'Johor approved more capacity'. The kicker still passes the WhatsApp test — a thoughtful reader could paste it alone and the structural point lands.

claims_NOT_changed_and_why: The Singapore charges date (27 Feb 2025) — verified across multiple cards; Stage 3 to confirm SPF press-release exact title.
BIS rule date (13 Jan 2025) and rescission date (13 May 2025) — Stage 3 to verify the Federal Register cites.

Johor 80+ projects, 5 GW — the brief acknowledges this is a range and uses the mid-conservative figure; Stage 3 (real web) should reconcile against the latest MIDA Annual Investment Performance Report before publication. Cards now say 'announced' (twice) and 'if all are built' to soften any operational claim.
No specific Chinese AI firm named — Stage 5 explicitly recommended not naming DeepSeek absent SPF charge-sheet text. Held.
No specific Malaysian DC operator named as a diversion actor — Stage 5 confirmed none has been publicly named; this stands.
S$390M vs US$390M figure omitted from cards — brief flagged the discrepancy and cards keep it out until Stage 3 (real web) resolves it. Held.
open_items_for_real_stage_3_before_publi: Confirm SPF press release exact title, date, charge wording, and the citizenship categories of the three accused (cards now name Singaporean/PR/Chinese national — Stage 3 must back-verify).
Confirm BIS Federal Register cites for both the Jan 2025 Diffusion Rule and the May 2025 rescission. Pull the latest BIS guidance text issued before 17 May 2026.
Reconcile MIDA Johor DC approval count and announced GW figure against the latest MIDA Annual Investment Performance Report (the brief uses a mid-conservative range).

Verify Microsoft/AWS Malaysia-DC geographic distribution (the synthesis dropped Microsoft and AWS from the Johor anchor-tenant list because Stage 3 catch indicated they are more Greater-KL/Selangor; back-verify there is no Microsoft or AWS Johor-specific DC investment that should be re-included).
Check whether any public Conference of Rulers / royal-decree statement is on file (almost certainly not for a trade-security topic, but record absence).

Check whether Suruhanjaya Tenaga has published a Peninsular peak-demand figure more recent than the brief's reference; the '~20 GW peak, ~5 GW DC = ~25%' arithmetic should hold up but worth a sanity check.

stage_score_rationales.pa: v1 self-scored 78; Stage 6 raises to 82 after the corrections above land cleanly without introducing unverified specifics. Capped at 82 because Stage 3 has not run against live web — the post-cutoff and primary-source-cite items in 'open_items' remain.
stage_score_rationales.ba: Stage 2 bias_score: 24. ba (quality, not amount) = 100 - 24 = 76. The corrections applied here address all four cardinal-sin flags Stage 2 raised; the bias amount in the v2 cards should be lower than 24 if Stage 2 re-ran on v2.
stage_score_rationales.fc: Stage 3 factual_accuracy_score: 74. The Google-Selangor catch was the single biggest correction. Capped at 74 because the in-house Stage 3 did not have web search; the score will adjust when real Stage 3 runs against primary sources.
stage_score_rationales.sr: Self-scored 82. The synthesis tagged every revision with one of CORRECTED / REPHRASED / INTRODUCED with explicit traceability. The single INTRODUCED change (the citizenship categories in the hook) sits at a verification bar Stage 3 will close. Posture remains conservative; no card asserts more than the brief supports.
stage_score_rationales.af: Stage 4 completeness: 62. Stage 4 surfaced the strongest single editorial gain in this round (the Tier 2 rescission framing and the Putrajaya-agency reframe). Stored as optional per current 4-stage convention.
stage_score_rationales.ct: Stage 5 courage: 62, confidence inflation -0.15. The under-claim direction was correctly identified; the corrected cards now land closer to the harshest accurate phrasing. Stored as optional per current 4-stage convention.

final_score_formula: (pa + ba + fc + sr) / 4 = (82 + 76 + 74 + 82) / 4 = 78.5. Follows current 4-stage convention; af/ct retained as optional values but excluded from finalScore arithmetic per CLAUDE.md.

_self_check.every_revision_traceable: Yes — each entry in revisions_applied has a change_type tag of CORRECTED / REPHRASED / INTRODUCED with explicit Stage attribution and rationale.
_self_check.introduced_specifics: Only the citizenship categories (Singaporean / PR / Chinese national) in the hook were INTRODUCED; sourced to the brief and widely-reported SPF announcement. Stage 3 must close verification against the SPF charge-sheet wording before publish.
_self_check.overclaim_check_v2: v2 reframe was tested against the playbook's 'value restructuring' form and passes. v2 view's '14 months' and 'no Malaysian case has been announced' are verifiable arithmetic and verifiable absence. No new overclaim introduced.
_self_check.underclaim_check_v2: Stage 2 and Stage 5 both said v1 was under-claiming. v2 governance card now states 'no Malaysian company has been publicly named' directly. v2 view lands on the 14-month vacuum. v2 hook adds the citizenship categories. Net: v2 is sharper without overstepping.
_self_check.misleading_framing_check_v2: Stage 2's biggest catch (headline 'Johor kept building' implying reactive defiance) and Stage 3's biggest catch (Google-in-Johor) both corrected. The structural-dependence framing in the new headline is verifiable.
_self_check.unverified_detail_check_v2: v2 does not introduce DeepSeek, does not name any MY DC operator as a diversion actor, does not assert a dollar figure for the SPF case, does not assert a USD denomination on the suspect transactions. The single INTRODUCED detail (citizenship categories) is at the lower-bar 'widely reported' tier and Stage 3 will close it.
_self_check.3R_check: None of the revisions touch race / religion / royalty content. Issue remains pure trade-security-economic.
_self_check.process_not_people_check_v2: v2 reframe and view both target the structural mechanic (STA 2010 unused, 14-month vacuum) rather than any individual. Tengku Zafrul is named in his then-official capacity; no characterisation of bad faith.
